projects.name: Homelab Foundation
projects.description: Set up TrueNAS SCALE on a Dell Optiplex 3070 SFF with a dual-pool storage layout — a 3TB HDD pool (tank) for media and a separate SSD pool (storage) for app configs. All services run as Docker containers managed through Dockge, exposed to the internet securely via Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnels with no open ports required.

projects.name: Media Ecosystem
projects.description: Full media acquisition stack: Jellyfin handles streaming with Intel QuickSync hardware transcoding, a full plugin suite (Intro Skipper, Merge Versions, Trickplay previews, Ultrachromic theme), and Usenet via SABnzbd as the primary download client. Sonarr, Radarr, and Prowlarr automate discovery and organization with Recyclarr keeping TRaSH Guide quality profiles in sync. All download traffic routes through NordVPN via Gluetun.

projects.name: Photo & Game Libraries
projects.description: Immich provides fully self-hosted photo and video backup with SMB external library support and Authentik SSO sign-in. Romm manages a retro game library of 2,319 titles across 19 platforms with cover art and metadata pulled from IGDB, running on a MariaDB backend.

projects.name: Security & Access Layer
projects.description: Tailscale VPN deployed on all devices with subnet routing and MagicDNS — TrueNAS and internal tools now access exclusively through the tailnet, off the public internet. Authentik SSO deployed at auth.gonzotech.us providing single sign-on across Jellyfin, Immich, Nextcloud, and Cloudflare Zero Trust with Google social login support.

projects.name: Cloud Storage Suite
projects.description: Nextcloud deployed at cloud.gonzotech.us for self-hosted file sync, CalDAV/CardDAV, and cloud storage — a full Google Drive/iCloud replacement. Collabora Online (CODE) running at office.gonzotech.us provides browser-based document editing integrated directly into Nextcloud. Both backed by Postgres + Redis and protected by Authentik SSO.

projects.name: Infrastructure Hardening
projects.description: Cloudflared isolated into its own Docker stack so ARR stack crashes no longer kill public subdomains. A shared external proxy Docker network connects all stacks cleanly. Recyclarr syncs TRaSH Guide profiles on a schedule to keep Radarr and Sonarr quality settings tuned without manual intervention.

projects.name: AdGuard Home
projects.description: AdGuard Home deployed as a Docker container for network-wide DNS ad-blocking. Container is up and accessible via Tailscale — router DNS config still needs to be pointed at the server to go fully live.

projects.name: Media Pipeline Tuning
projects.description: Several housekeeping items in flight: Radarr custom format min score and file size limits need adjustment (YTS releases currently flagged as low quality), Romm config file volume path needs a fix to clear the startup warning, and a Radarr Manual Import pass on /data/torrents/movies is needed to register existing files. Jellyfin Intro Skipper plugin repo URL also changed and needs updating.

projects.name: 3-2-1 Automated Backup
projects.description: Priority #1 project. TrueNAS CloudSync to Backblaze B2 for all app configs and critical data. ZFS snapshots on the tank pool for point-in-time recovery. Without off-site backup the lab is temporary — this converts it into a real system.

projects.name: Vaultwarden
projects.description: Self-hosted Bitwarden-compatible password manager. Clear community standard for self-hosting. Deploying this enables proper secret management — .env files and credentials can be moved out of plaintext and into a vault.

projects.name: Observability Stack (Grafana + Loki)
projects.description: Promtail → Loki → Grafana for log aggregation and dashboards across all containers. Industry-standard monitoring stack that'll make diagnosing issues much faster. Grafana also enables custom dashboards for storage, network, and service health.

projects.name: Home Assistant
projects.description: Gold-standard home automation platform. Deploying this before deciding on N8N vs Node-RED for automation workflows. Pairs naturally with Frigate NVR for camera AI object detection down the road.

projects.name: Hardware Upgrade (Jonsbo N5)
projects.description: The Optiplex 3070 has been a great starting point. Upgrading to a purpose-built NAS chassis (Jonsbo N5 or similar) when the current hardware becomes an actual bottleneck — more drive bays, PCIe slots, and ECC RAM support will open the door to larger ZFS pools and more demanding workloads.

automation.name: Automated Media Downloads
automation.description: Sonarr and Radarr monitor for new releases and trigger downloads automatically — SABnzbd handles Usenet as the primary client (Newshosting, 30 connections, SSL) with qBittorrent as the torrent fallback. Once downloaded, files are moved, renamed, hardlinked into the media library, and picked up by Jellyfin — no manual steps required.

automation.name: Quality Profile Sync (Recyclarr)
automation.description: Recyclarr runs on a schedule to keep Radarr and Sonarr aligned with TRaSH Guide quality profiles. Ensures release scoring, preferred formats (HD Bluray + WEB for Radarr, WEB-1080p for Sonarr), and quality cutoffs stay tuned without digging through settings manually. Min custom format score set to -10000.

automation.name: Cloudflare Tunnel Management
automation.description: A dedicated Cloudflared container maintains persistent tunnels for all public subdomains — completely isolated from application stacks so an ARR or Nextcloud restart never affects public access. Adding a new public service is a single tunnel entry in the Cloudflare dashboard with zero firewall or router changes.

automation.name: SSO Authentication (Authentik)
automation.description: Authentik handles single sign-on across the entire stack — Jellyfin, Immich, Nextcloud, and Cloudflare Zero Trust all authenticate through auth.gonzotech.us. Google social login enabled. Admin roles assigned via Authentik groups, eliminating per-service account management.

automation.name: Uptime Monitoring (Uptime Kuma)
automation.description: Uptime Kuma checks every public service and internal Docker socket at regular intervals and sends alerts on failure. The public status page at status.gonzotech.us shows live health of the full stack — useful for sharing uptime with anyone who accesses the services.
